summary: Moderate-quality recording of a solid Dead show with standout Bob Weir vocals.
review: Both reviewers praise the energy of the performance, especially noting that Bob Weir sang like never before, possibly spurred by personal events. The show is described as classic Grateful Dead – unpredictable yet reliably good – but no details about the recording source or sound quality are provided.

ai_rating.rationale: Two moderate (3‑star) reviews highlight a decent musical performance with notable vocal work, but give no information on recording quality, leading to an average overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers did not comment on the audio source or clarity; no technical assessment available.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Described as classic Grateful Dead – unpredictable and reliably good, with extra emotional intensity after Bob's dog Otis died.

band_member_comments.Bob: Reviewers note Bob Weir’s vocals were exceptional and unusually emotive for this show.
